Class MoveResult

java.lang.Object
core.MoveResult
public class MoveResult extends Object
Encapsulates the outcome of an attempted move or action within the game. This class provides details on whether the move was successful, if any piece was captured, and whether the move resulted in a game-winning state.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Class

    static enum 
    MoveResult.Status
    Represents the specific status or reason for a move's outcome.

  • Constructor Details

    • MoveResult

      public MoveResult(MoveResult.Status status, Piece capturedPiece, boolean isWon)
      Constructs a new MoveResult with the specified details.
      Parameters:
      status - the outcome status of the move
      capturedPiece - the piece that was captured, or null if none
      isWon - true if this move won the game, false otherwise

  • Method Details

    • success

      public static MoveResult success(Piece captured, boolean won)
      Creates a successful MoveResult.
      Parameters:
      captured - the piece captured during the move, or null if no capture occurred
      won - true if the move triggered a win condition
      Returns:
      a MoveResult representing a successful action

    • failure

      public static MoveResult failure(MoveResult.Status status)
      Creates a failed MoveResult with a specific error status.
      Parameters:
      status - the reason for the failure (e.g., INVALID_MOVE, WRONG_TURN)
      Returns:
      a MoveResult representing a failed action

    • getStatus

      public MoveResult.Status getStatus()
      Retrieves the status of the move.
      Returns:
      the move's status enum

    • getCapturedPiece

      public Piece getCapturedPiece()
      Retrieves the piece that was captured during this move, if any.
      Returns:
      the captured Piece, or null if no piece was captured

    • isSuccess

      public boolean isSuccess()
      Checks if the move was successful.
      Returns:
      true if the status is SUCCESS, false otherwise

    • isWon

      public boolean isWon()
      Checks if this move resulted in winning the game.
      Returns:
      true if the move won the game, false otherwise
